title: Scan .claude/skills and .claude/commands for doc-anchor drift
context: Agent-facing documentation (skills, commands) reference decisions but fell outside the main doc-scan walk. If a skill preaches a superseded decision, it silently misleads every future session without explicit warning. These files need drift-checking like any other prose tied to the decision graph. Touches invariant(s): con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Extended markdownDocs() to explicitly scan .claude/skills and .claude/commands directories after the main walk, subjecting agent-facing prose to the same doc-anchor-stale detection as technical specifications.
consequences: Skills and commands with stale decision anchors now surface doc-anchor-stale findings
Agents no longer unknowingly follow superseded doctrine across sessions
Fable-mode verdict playbook pinned to dec_58f016807f as dogfood — if that call is ever superseded, the skill fires stale-anchor warning instead of preaching outdated doctrine
Quality nudge added to hunch_record_decision: advisory (one line, never a gate) for unattacked records or rejections without "revisit if" flip conditions
alternatives_rejected: Leave agent prose out of drift-check: silent staleness in prose agents follow every session — unacceptable risk
Scan dot-dirs generally in markdownDocs: surfaces noise from unrelated config; explicit scan is clearer intent
Require manual review process: async gap between decisions and prose updates makes this unworkable at scale

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Leave agent prose out of drift-check: silent staleness in prose agents follow every session — unacceptable risk
Scan dot-dirs generally in markdownDocs: surfaces noise from unrelated config; explicit scan is clearer intent
Require manual review process: async gap between decisions and prose updates makes this unworkable at scale
